title: The Thirde Order of Seynt Franceys for the Brethren and Susters of the Order of Penitentis
summary: The repository artifact is a local CME-submodule representation of University of Michigan item 3rdFranRule, produced in 1999 by scanning and ScanWorx OCR from Walter W. Seton's contribution to EETS Original Series 148 (1914). Seton transcribed the English rule from the former Pennant and Seton manuscript, now National Library of Wales MS 22873B, fols. 2r-15v. That Middle English version translates the rule incorporated in Pope Nicholas IV's 1289 bull Supra montem. The U-M selection retains the edition's component title page, chapter table, and twenty-four-chapter English text but omits Seton's introduction, endnotes, glossary, and the manuscript's following Latin extract. A complete scan of the 1914 composite book is public through Internet Archive; within it Seton reproduces only manuscript fols. 9v and 15v, not a complete manuscript facsimile.
review_notes: The cataloged work is the Middle English rule on NLW MS 22873B fols. 2r-15v, not the whole manuscript, Nicholas IV's Latin source, or the complete 1914 book.
The current NLW catalog resolves the XML's historical 'whereabouts unknown' note: the former Pennant and Seton witness is extant as NLW MS 22873B.
Seton's volume is composite: R. W. Chambers edits the courtesy book, while Walter W. Seton edits the two Franciscan rules. Only Seton has responsibility for 3rdFranRule.
The U-M encoding was generated by scanning and OCR; no item-specific statement establishing full manual correction was found.
U-M's current No Copyright-United States statement and the restrictive 1999 XML availability notice are preserved separately rather than reconciled by assumption.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for 3rdFranRule
entities.description: Repository XML supplied through the pinned CME submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item 3rdFranRule
entities.description: A 1999 electronic text created at the University of Michigan Humanities Text Initiative by scanning Seton's printed component and performing OCR with ScanWorx. It encodes the Middle English rule and selected edition framing, not the whole book or manuscript.
notes: The XML language declaration names Latin but omits primary Middle English.
The text preserves manuscript folio-side milestones 2r-15v and print-page milestones 45-55.
Print footnote information is generally represented with CORR, ADD, and DEL; one residual NOTE1 remains.

entities.label: Seton 1914 edition of two fifteenth-century Franciscan rules
alternate_labels: A Fifteenth-Century Courtesy Book and Two Fifteenth-Century Franciscan Rules
entities.description: The immediate printed source is Walter W. Seton's portion of a composite EETS volume. The 3rdFranRule component comprises an introduction (pp. 25-41), bibliography (p. 41), the English rule (pp. 45-55), the adjacent Latin Beatus Franciscus text from the same manuscript (pp. 55-57), notes (pp. 58-59), and a shared glossary (pp. 126-127).

bibliographic.citation: A Fifteenth-Century Courtesy Book, ed. R. W. Chambers, and Two Fifteenth-Century Franciscan Rules, ed. Walter W. Seton, Early English Text Society, Original Series 148 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., and Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press, 1914).
bibliographic.title: A Fifteenth-Century Courtesy Book and Two Fifteenth-Century Franciscan Rules
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 148
bibliographic.publisher: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., and Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press, for the Early English Text Society

notes: Chambers edits only the opening courtesy-book work; Seton edits both Franciscan rules and supplies their apparatus.

entities.label: National Library of Wales MS 22873B
entities.description: A nineteen-leaf illustrated vellum devotional manuscript containing the English rule at fols. 2r-15v, an added Latin extract from Bernardine de Bustis at fols. 15v-17, and prayers and miniatures at fols. 17v-19. It was formerly in Thomas Pennant's Downing collection, was purchased by Seton after the March 1913 Sotheby's sale, and is now held by the National Library of Wales.

date_statements.label: Seton's assessment of English-rule hand
date_statements.value: latter half of the fifteenth century
date_statements.notes: Older editorial assessment retained separately from the current NLW acquisition description.

holding.notes: The exact NLW component appears in the official Archives and Manuscripts result page under 'Rule of the Third Order of St Francis'.

physical_description.extent: 19 leaves; the English rule occupies fols. 2r-15v
notes: The English rule is in a good bastard secretary script; Seton describes it as a neat, legible English hand with generally nineteen lines per page.
Three full-page miniatures survive at fols. 1v, 17v, and 18v; leaves for five intervening Offices were apparently cut out for their miniatures.
The current NLW catalog calls the Latin addition contemporary and in similar script; Seton calls it a later addition in a different, smaller, apparently Italian hand.
Seton's introductory sentence says the English version occupies leaves 2-14, but his edition and the current catalog show that the ending continues through fol. 15v.

entities.label: Seton 1914 printed facsimiles of Pennant MS fols. 9v and 15v
entities.description: Two monochrome plates reproducing selected pages of the manuscript within EETS OS 148; they are not a complete manuscript surrogate.
entities.reproduction_format: Two printed monochrome facsimile plates, subsequently visible in digital scans of the edition

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of EETS Original Series 148
entities.description: A complete 150-page digital scan of the composite 1914 volume from the University of Toronto Kelly Library copy.

date_statements.label: digital availability
date_statements.value: Date not established in reviewed item metadata

entities.reproduction_format: PDF, OCR text, EPUB, and page-image derivatives

entities.label: Nicholas IV, Supra montem
entities.description: The Latin papal bull promulgating a rule for brothers and sisters of the Order of Penance; Seton identifies its rule text as the source translated by the Middle English version.
notes: This entity represents the source work and document tradition, not a claim that the English translator used one identified surviving Latin codex.

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright
entities.description: The April 2026 revision of the Copyright Office's duration guidance, used only to support this manifest's United States rights assessment for the 1914 edition; it is not part of the textual transmission lineage.

agents.name: R. W. Chambers
notes: Editor of the courtesy-book component, not of 3rdFranRule.

assertion.notes: The header, identifier, title, structure, and production statement identify the U-M item; a current downloadable source XML was not separately checksummed.

subject.description: Electronic title page, chapter table, and twenty-four-chapter English rule

object.description: Seton's component title page and English text

scope.notes: The encoding omits Seton's introduction, endnotes, glossary, and the Latin Beatus Franciscus text printed at pp. 55-57.
Seton also prints the adjacent Latin extract at pp. 55-57, but that is outside the cataloged English work and the U-M encoding.
The relation identifies the translated source work; it does not identify the English translator's physical Latin exemplar.
The plates do not constitute a complete facsimile.

subject.description: Illustration plates within the Third Order component
Repository work and its linked manifest rights analysis

scope.notes: This is an evidentiary rights-analysis dependency, not a textual derivation edge.

work_portion.label: The Rule of the Third Order of St Francis
locators: printed manuscript plates reproduce fols. 9v and 15v
notes: These are two selected manuscript pages, not the complete NLW MS 22873B codex.

access.format: Two plates within PDF page images

access.access_method: NLW Archives and Manuscripts catalog result for shelfmark 22873B
Direct NLW annual-report PDF containing acquisition record 1992036
Register for a reader's ticket and request the shelfmark through NLW Archives and Manuscripts for Reading Room consultation; final issue is subject to condition and repository rules
Request item-specific photography or digital imaging from NLW; availability depends on copyright and conditional restrictions

rights.component: The medieval English and Latin texts, Seton's 1914 transcription and apparatus, and the two published manuscript plates
rights.basis: The edition was first published in 1914, more than the 95-year maximum term described by the U.S. Copyright Office for pre-1978 statutory copyrights; U-M also currently labels the item public domain in the United States.

rights.component: Materials presented in U-M CME item 3rdFranRule
rights.basis: The current item page expressly states that the materials are in the public domain and applies No Copyright-United States.
notes: The 1999 embedded availability notice permits personal, research, and teaching copies but forbids redistribution by commercial or not-for-profit content providers; whether the current statement supersedes that notice is unresolved.

rights.component: The local U-M-derived XML markup and transcription
rights.basis: The underlying edition and current U-M item are public domain in the United States, but the checked-in XML embeds an express redistribution restriction.

rights.component: Internet Archive's digital scan and generated derivatives
rights.basis: The scanned 1914 edition is public domain in the United States, but the reviewed Internet Archive item metadata supplies no item-specific rights or license field for the scan derivatives.

rights.component: Modern photographs, scans, or other reproductions supplied by the National Library of Wales
rights.basis: No item-specific reusable image set or license was verified. NLW states that supplied copies may not be reproduced without written permission and that restricted reuse requires a license; underlying-work status does not determine modern-image terms.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript contractions and uncertain or supplied readings
editorial_practices.representation: Universally recognized contractions are expanded silently; editorial departures use italics, supplied letters or words are italicized in square brackets, Latin is italicized, and more elaborate Latin expansions are bracketed.
editorial_practices.description: Seton's note before the text states his expansion, italics, bracket, supply, and Latin conventions for the Third Order rule.
editorial_practices.scope: English rule pp. 45-55 and Latin extract pp. 55-57

editorial_practices.source_feature: Composite manuscript containing the English rule, Latin addition, Offices, miniatures, and missing leaves
editorial_practices.representation: Seton transcribes the English rule and Latin addition, describes the Offices and miniatures, and reproduces only fols. 9v and 15v as plates.
editorial_practices.description: The printed component is a selective scholarly presentation of the manuscript, not a complete transcription or facsimile.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Red chapter headings and some proper names in the manuscript
editorial_practices.representation: Printed monochrome headings and ordinary typographic display
editorial_practices.description: Seton's description records red manuscript writing, while the inspected scan shows a monochrome printed representation.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Seton's component title page, introduction, bibliography, English text, Latin extract, notes, and glossary
editorial_practices.representation: The electronic item retains the component title page, table, and English rule; it omits the introduction, endnotes, Latin extract following the English text, and glossary.
editorial_practices.description: The U-M item is a work-level selection from the larger printed component and composite volume.

editorial_practices.representation: Textual information from print footnotes is generally incorporated through CORR, ADD, and DEL tags where possible; one note is retained when integration is not possible.
editorial_practices.description: The encoding converts most print-apparatus information into inline structural markup rather than preserving Seton's footnote layout.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/3rdFranRule.xml, header, editorial declaration, and full text.
evidence.summary: Supplies the 1999 U-M identity and production statement, 1914 source, former witness designation, omissions, tag conversion practices, embedded provider terms, folio and page milestones, and selected English text.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, CME item 3rdFranRule.
evidence.locator: Item information, rights statement, full-text action, and contents
evidence.summary: Confirms Seton editorship, the 1914 London edition, public full text, twenty-four chapter contents, stable handle, and current Public Domain / No Copyright-United States assertion.

evidence.citation: R. W. Chambers and Walter W. Seton, eds., A Fifteenth-Century Courtesy Book and Two Fifteenth-Century Franciscan Rules, EETS OS 148 (1914), Internet Archive courtesybook00unknuoft.
evidence.locator: Title page and contents; pp. 25-41, 45-59, and 126-127; plates reproducing manuscript fols. 9v and 15v
evidence.summary: The inspected complete scan establishes edition responsibility and bibliography, the source-work analysis, manuscript provenance and physical description, direct transcription, editorial conventions, English and Latin content, notes, glossary, and two selected manuscript facsimiles.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item courtesybook00unknuoft and item metadata.
evidence.summary: Provides a retrievable 150-page scan and derivatives of the University of Toronto Kelly Library copy; metadata names scanner uoft1 and supplies no item-specific rights or license field.

evidence.citation: Wellcome Collection, A Fifteenth-Century Courtesy Book and Two Fifteenth-Century Franciscan Rules, record su9y2v8b.
evidence.summary: Confirms the composite 1914 book's publication, physical extent, Chambers and Seton contributors, EETS series, and three-work contents.

evidence.citation: National Library of Wales Archives and Manuscripts, Rule of the Third Order of St Francis, NLW MS 22873B.
evidence.summary: Identifies the extant witness and current shelfmark, English-rule folios, script, adjacent Latin addition, Offices, missing leaves, three miniatures, decoration, two Seton facsimile folios, and the reported identical 1510 de Worde print.
notes: The current test-host result supplied the description in indexed output but intermittently failed on direct reload; claims were cross-checked with the NLW annual report and Seton scan.

evidence.citation: National Library of Wales, Annual Report 1991-92, acquisition 1992036.
evidence.summary: Records acquisition of NLW MS 22873B, describes the unique English translation and c. 1500 manuscript, and confirms former ownership by Thomas Pennant at Downing.

evidence.citation: Institut de recherche et d'histoire des textes, Telma - Chartes, Nicholas IV, Supra montem, record 154463.
evidence.locator: Date, document analysis, and Latin transcription
evidence.summary: Provides a research-institute charter record and Latin transcription of Nicholas IV's rule-bearing bull, dated 18 August 1289.

evidence.citation: National Library of Wales, Manuscripts collection and Reading Room access guidance.
evidence.summary: States that manuscript users obtain a reader's ticket and consult requested manuscripts in the Reading Room, with surrogate or restricted access possible according to age and condition.

evidence.citation: National Library of Wales, Requesting copies.
evidence.summary: Offers item-specific copying services for a fee, subject to copyright and conditional restrictions, and states that supplied copies may not be reproduced without written permission.
States that otherwise restricted reuse of NLW digital reproductions requires a license, may require rights-holder permission, and is subject to fees and license terms.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright, revised April 2026.
evidence.locator: p. 2, works already under statutory protection before 1978
evidence.summary: Describes a 95-year maximum total term for pre-1978 statutory copyrights and states that U.S.-published works before 1 January 1931 are public domain; together with U-M's item statement, this supports the U.S. assessment for the 1914 edition.

open_questions.question: Does U-M's current No Copyright-United States statement supersede the 1999 XML restriction against redistribution by commercial or not-for-profit content providers?
next_steps: Obtain a written interpretation from U-M before redistributing the XML or a close derivative.
Continue to distinguish copyright status from provider-contract status.

open_questions.question: Does NLW offer an existing complete digital or microfilm surrogate of MS 22873B, and what item-specific access and reuse terms would apply?
next_steps: Ask NLW by shelfmark about existing surrogates, conservation limits, delivery format, fees, and item-specific license terms.
Do not treat Seton's two plates or the public 1914 book scan as a complete manuscript facsimile.

open_questions.question: Is the 1510 Wynkyn de Worde print copied from NLW MS 22873B, from a common exemplar, or otherwise related?
next_steps: Inspect and collate an identified surviving copy of STC 19596.
Do not add a directed derivation edge from the current catalog's statement that its text is identical.

open_questions.question: How should the date and hand of the Latin addition, and the exact codicological extent of the English rule, be reconciled between Seton's 1914 description and the current NLW catalog?
next_steps: Request current curatorial clarification or inspect the manuscript directly.
Preserve both descriptions rather than silently selecting Seton's 'later Italian hand' or the catalog's 'contemporary similar script'.
